data.title: OpenAI pushes monetization efforts further with new $200/month plan
data.description:
• Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) OpenAI is testing the limits of monetizing its artificial intelligence models as it announced a $200 per month subscription plan for its new ChatGPT Pro option.
  • The Sam Altman-led startup revealed the new plan during a livestream today in what is Day 1 of its "12 Days of OpenAI."
  • "Today we're also adding ChatGPT Pro, a new plan that allows us to offer the best of our models & tools at scale, including unlimited access to OpenAI o1 and a Pro-only version of o1 that thinks longer for even more reliable responses," OpenAI posted on X.
  • ChatGPT Pro offers unlimited access to ChatGPT 4o and o1, its newest reasoning model that is capable of completing more complex tasks. o1 is also capable of completing advanced math and coding problems.
  • The Pro plan is available starting today and is quite a jump in price from its Plus plan, which costs $20 per month.

data.title: Equity REITs continue to reduce NAV discount
data.description:
Equity real estate investment trusts in the U.S. that are publicly listed ended Nov. 29 at a median 6.5% discount as compared to their consensus net asset value per share estimates, according to S&P Global Market Intelligence data.

The discount was 2.8 percentage points below the median discount of 9.3% as of Nov. 1.

The hotel sector continued to be the largest median discount to net asset value (NAV) at 21.6%, down compared to a median discount of 27.0% in the prior month.

The other sectors that preceeded were timber at 18.6%, farmland at 17.1% and office at 14.6%.

Datacenter REITs traded at the largest median premium to NAV, at 27.6%, followed by the healthcare sector.

Industrial Logistics Properties Trust (ILPT) traded at the largest discount to NAV among all U.S. REITs with at least $200 million in market capitalization, according to the report. Hudson Pacific Properties (NYSE:HPP) took the second spot.

Seven of the 10 public REITs with a market capitalization of at least $200 million that traded at the largest premiums, were from the healthcare sector, including Welltower (NYSE:WELL)

data.title: Classic meme stocks pop on cryptic tweet from Roaring Kitty
data.description:
Shares of GameStop (NYSE:GME) rose more than 10% in Thursday afternoon trading after Keith Gill, the stock influencer known as Roaring Kitty, posted a cryptic image on social media site X, formerly known as Twitter. Shares of AMC Entertainment (AMC) also rose more than 6%.

GameStop (GME) shares were momentarily halted for volatility, but resumed trading shortly thereafter.

Gill, known for his cryptic posts, often uses social media platforms like YouTube and Reddit to disclose his trading positions. The influencer boasts a cult following among some investors.

The influencer gained popularity in 2021 after igniting a frenzy of activity in shares of GameStop (GME) which led to an unexpected surge in the struggling gaming retailer’s shares.

Other popular meme stocks include AMC (AMC) and technology firm Blackberry (BB) which have also seen their share prices jump after getting backed by an army of online investors.

The Roaring Kitty image:

data.title: Dell COO says it's provided 'thousands' of GPUs in AI servers to xAI in new interview
data.description:
Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) Chief Operating Officer Jeff Clarke said in a new interview the company has provided "thousands" of GPUs via its artificial intelligence servers to Elon Musk's generative AI startup xAI.

“We’ve gone from a blank piece of paper to deploying, at scale, tens of thousands of GPUs in a handful of months,” Clarke said in an interview with Bloomberg on Thursday. “That cluster continues to be built out and we’re distinguishing ourselves.”

Dell shares rose fractionally in midday trading on Thursday.

In June, Musk confirmed that Dell, along with Super Micro Computer (SMCI), would provide servers for the supercomputer that xAI is building.

In November, xAI raised $6B in new funding that valued the company at $50B. Some of the new funding is going towards xAI's massive data center in Memphis, where it will use 100,000 of Nvidia's (NVDA) H100 GPUs. The process to build it took 122 days.

Musk said in July he would bring the idea of Tesla (TSLA) investing $5B into xAI to the electric vehicle maker's board of directors after the idea received nearly two-thirds of "yes" votes in an online poll.

data.title: Stuffed with success: Build-A-Bear shares rally on record Q3 results
data.description:
The expansion into international markets and third-party retailers continues to pay off for Build-A-Bear Workshop (NYSE:BBW) as the company reported its best-ever third quarter performance and a step closer to four consecutive years of record revenues.

Shares were launched as much as 22% higher to a record high.

The toy retailer beat both profit and revenue expectations with an 11% increase in Q3 sales, driving revenue to a record high of $119.4M. And while e-commerce sales continue to run below expectations, a commercial and international franchise revenue shot up 39% for a combined $9.9M.

During the quarter, Build-A-Bear (BBW) opened 17 new global locations, resulting in a total of 565 global workshops with 362 corporate-managed, 123 partner-operated, and 80 franchisee-managed.

On the balance sheet, the company increased its cash and cash equivalents by $4.2M to $29.0M, had no borrowings under its revolving credit facility, and increased its inventory by 9.8% to $70.8M.

Looking ahead to 2024, Build-A-Bear (BBW) expects revenue to be between $489M and $495M, representing growth on a low-single-digit percentage basis at the midpoint. This compares to the consensus estimate of $493.6M.

data.title: Billionaire Frank McCourt is preparing a 'people's bid' for TikTok - Axios
GSK in agreement with Muna to develop Alzheimer's therapies
data.description:
• GSK (NYSE:GSK) has entered into a agreement with privately held Muna Therapeutics to to develop medicines for Alzheimer's disease.
  • The arrangement will use Muna's MiND-MAP platform, which "applies spatial transcriptomics to brain samples from Alzheimer’s disease patients, cognitively resilient individuals, healthy controls, and centenarians with and without cognitive impairment."
  • The two companies will evaluate postmortem brain samples with spatial transcriptomics to identify and validate potential new drug targets.
  • Terms call for Muna to receive an upfront payment of €33.5M (~$35.4M). In addition, the Danish biotech will be eligible for up to €140M (~$147.9M) per target in milestone payments, plus tiered royalties on net sales of products.
  • GSK will head drug development and is responsible for preclinical activities, clinical development, manufacturing, and commercialization of any candidates.

data.title: SoundHound AI skyrockets following investor conference, taco shop deal
data.description:
• SoundHound AI (NASDAQ:SOUN) shares soared as much as 30% during Thursday market action.
  • The day prior, SoundHound CFO Nitesh Sharan showcased his company's artificial intelligence-powered, conversational voice products at the UBS Global AI and Technology conference.
  • The Santa Clara, Calif.-based company also announced a new deal with Torchy's Tacos today, which are rolling out SoundHound's voice AI Smart Ordering product at its 130 restaurant locations.
  • "This voice AI ordering capability manages 100% of incoming calls, including handling multiple calls and orders simultaneously," SoundHound said in a statement. "It not only takes food orders but also answers common questions about menu items, specials, store hours, and allergen information."
  • SoundHound's voice AI offerings are now deployed at more than 10,000 restaurant locations.
  • SoundHound shares have now climbed more than 500% year to date.
  • A couple other AI stocks were increasing during Thursday trading as well. BigBear.ai (NYSE:BBAI) had surged 15% by afternoon trading. C3.ai (NYSE:AI) was up 1.5%. However, Tempus AI (NASDAQ:TEM) and Klaviyo (NYSE:KVYO) had both slid nearly 4%.

data.title: With just a couple weeks left in 2024, here are the top 10 S&P 500 performers YTD
data.description:
Wall Street’s broader market averages have surged to new record highs, with the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) reaching an impressive 6,094 on Thursday, marking a fresh all-time high for the index.

As the year draws to a close with only a few weeks remaining in trading, it's an ideal time to take a closer look at the top-performing S&P 500 stocks of 2024. Outlined below is a breakdown of which companies have stood out in this year where the S&P 500 has jumped 28.2%.

Top 10 S&P 500 Performing Stocks of 2024

No. 10: Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG), +119.3% YTD.

No. 9: Howmet Aerospace (HWM), +122.7% YTD.

No. 8: Targa Resources Corp. (TRGP), +128.9% YTD.

No. 7: United Airlines Holding (UAL), +152.1% YTD.

No. 6: Texas Pacific Land Corporation (TPL), +159.5% YTD.

No. 5: Axon Enterprise (AXON), +166.3% YTD.

No. 4: GE Vernova (GEV), +167.4% YTD.

No. 3: Nvidia (NVDA), +194.2% YTD.

No. 2: Palantir Technologies (PLTR), +320.4% YTD.

No. 1: Vistra Corp. (VST), +322.2% YTD.

Moreover, see what Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings sit at for the above 10 mentioned stocks.

SA Quant Grades: CEG 3.44, HWM 3.45, TRGP 3.47, UAL 4.97, TPL (no rating), AXON 3.49, GEV (no rating), NVDA 3.49, PLTR 3.49, and VST 3.47.

data.title: Jumia orders totalled 4.3M for two months ended November 30, up 18% from last year; Shares surge
data.description:
• Jumia Technologies AG (NYSE:JMIA) announced on Thursday, the preliminary unaudited results of selected KPIs for the two months ended November 30, 2024.
  • In the two months ended November 30, 2024, orders totalled 4.3 million, up 18% year-over-year, with 62% of orders placed during Jumia's Black Friday event in November.
  • The number of customers who placed an order increased by 9% and Gross Merchandise Volume on a constant currency basis increased by 33% in the two months ended November 30, 2024.
  • All reported preliminary KPIs exclude results from South Africa and Tunisia, reflecting the company's recently announced exit from these markets by year-end 2024.
  • Jumia Technologies (JMIA) shares rose more than 21% in Thursday afternoon trading.
